summary: Two-way star rating feature tested end-to-end on web preview (mobile viewport 390x844). Shipper-rates-driver UI flow fully verified: card renders with 5 interactive stars (rate-driver-star-1..5), review input (driver-review-input) appears only after star selection, submit button (submit-driver-rating) submits successfully, card switches to read-only YOU RATED state with stars (driver-rated-star-1..5), the comment quote, and 'Thanks for your feedback!' message. Persistence verified: after page reload the read-only state is preserved and interactive form does NOT reappear. Shipper profile rating endpoint returns rating=5 for the demo shipper; code review of shipper-settings.tsx confirms the star pill renders rating.toFixed(2) (e.g. '5.00') next to the shipments badge. Driver-rates-shipper flow (summary.tsx) verified by code review only (driver delivery flow not reproducible in a single session) and matches the spec: uses api.rateShipper, StarRating with testIDPrefix=rate-shipper-star, review-input testID, summary-done-button label switches between 'Continue' / 'Skip & continue' / 'Submit & continue'. Backend regression light-touch: shipper-login OK, GET /api/shipper/shipments/{id} returns driver_rating field, rate-driver endpoint successfully updated driver.rating in profile from 5 to 4 after a 4-star vote in a previous run. NOTE: First test pass failed because the Metro/Expo bundle was cached without the new rating block; a `sudo supervisorctl restart expo` cleared it and all tests then passed. Main agent should be aware that future shipped feature code may require an Expo restart for the dev preview to pick it up (no actual app bug).
action_items: Optional polish: shipper-settings.tsx redirects to /login on direct deep-link because loadProfile calls getAuthToken() synchronously before AuthContext hydrates. shipper-tracking.tsx already handles this with an authRetryRef retry loop; consider applying the same pattern to shipper-settings.tsx so the profile rating pill is visible immediately on deep-link / hard reload. NOT a rating-feature bug.
Optional: WebSocket /ws/track/{id} returns 502 in preview environment. Falls back to HTTP polling correctly. Unrelated to rating feature.
critical_code_review_comments: StarRating component (src/components/StarRating.tsx) is clean, reusable, supports half-stars in readonly mode, uses TouchableOpacity with proper hitSlop and accessibility labels.
shipper-tracking.tsx rate card correctly gated on shipment.status === 'delivered' && shipment.driver, and switches to read-only on (shipment.driver_rating || rateDone).
summary.tsx correctly uses non-blocking try/catch around api.rateShipper so a rating failure never traps the driver; button label logic is correct (Continue / Skip & continue / Submit & continue).
seed_data_creation: Consumed shipment ef1e1615-c7fa-43dd-89da-dab1e1efade5 (SHP-8525) by submitting a 4-star rating with review. Three unrated delivered shipments remain: bedc918f, 4fd60537, 1eff59e1.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Rating feature is working end-to-end. If you observe the rate card not rendering on a delivered shipment, the first thing to try is `sudo supervisorctl restart expo` to clear the Metro cache. Auth token persistence on web uses localStorage key 'nadaruns_auth_token'. For deep-link tests, store token first then page.goto; shipper-tracking handles the hydration race but shipper-settings does not.
rca_of_issue: Initial run showed the rate-driver card missing despite all React conditions being satisfied. RCA: stale Metro bundle in the Expo dev server did not include the latest shipper-tracking.tsx rating JSX. Mitigation: `sudo supervisorctl restart expo` rebuilt the bundle. After restart all five testIDs (rate-driver-star-1..5), driver-review-input, submit-driver-rating, and (post-submit) driver-rated-star-1..5 were present and the full flow including persistence-on-reload passed.
